content.problemStatement:
The set of balanced binary strings are as follows:


The empty string is balanced.
If SSS is balanced, then 0+S+10 + S + 10+S+1 is balanced.
If S,TS, TS,T are balanced, then S+TS + TS+T is balanced.
Here +++ denotes string concatenation.

For example, 01,010101, 010101,0101 and 001100110011 are balanced, but 101010 and 010010010 are not.

Given a binary string SSS of length NNN, you can perform the following operation on it as many times as you want:


Choose a sorted subsequence TTT from SSS, and then delete it from SSS.
Formally, we can describe the operation as:


Choose an integer KKK (1‚Č§K‚Č§N1 \le K \le N1‚Č§K‚Č§N), and indices i1,i2,‚Ä¶,iki_1, i_2, \ldots, i_ki1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ik‚Äč, satisfying

1‚Č§i1<i2<‚Ä¶<ik‚Č§N1 \le i_1 < i_2 < \ldots < i_k \le N1‚Č§i1‚Äč<i2‚Äč<‚Ä¶<ik‚Äč‚Č§N
Sij‚Č§Sij+1S_{i_j} \le S_{i_{j + 1}}Sij‚Äč‚Äč‚Č§Sij+1‚Äč‚Äč


Delete the indices i1,i2,‚Ä¶iki_1, i_2, \ldots i_ki1‚Äč,i2‚Äč,‚Ä¶ik‚Äč from the string SSS simultaneously and concatenate the remaining parts of the string formed.
Find the **minimum number** of operations needed to make SSS balanced.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the binary string SSS.
The second line contains SSS - a binary string of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum number of operations to make SSS balanced.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : The given string is already balanced.
Test case 222 : We can delete the only character in SSS, and then it becomes the empty string, which is balanced.
